title: play kube: Add --detach=true option
body:
Add a way to keep `play kube` running in the foreground and terminating all pods
after receiving a a SIGINT or SIGTERM signal. Defaults to true to reflect the current behaviour.

I'm unsure whether this `--detach=true` option should rather be renamed to `--foreground=false`. I named it so for consistency with the similar feature in `docker-compose up`. Suggestions are welcome.

Fixes #14522

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
podman play kube has a new `--detach=false` option to stay in foreground and terminate the pods after receiving a Ctrl-C signal
```

comments.body:
Thanks @1player 

You have to add the option to the man pages.

--detach is correct. Matches podman run --detach for containers.
/approve

Turning this into a draft. Either I'm missing something, or most times `play kube` immediately quits after receiving a Ctrl-C, as if something else is catching the signal and calling os.Exit, so teardown never happens. 

Yet I did not have this issue yesterday with the exact same code, so I'm not sure what's happening.
I don't believe this is a Draft at this point.

> I don't believe this is a Draft at this point.

I'm not sure what you mean. As it stands, this PR is broken and I have no idea why, and I'm not sure when I can spend more time to figure out what's going on. Shall I close it instead?
No we can take this over if you have a lack of time.
@umohnani8 @ashley-cui @vrothberg @giuseppe PTAL

> No we can take this over if you have a lack of time.

Feel free to do so. I am curious to know why catching SIGINT doesn't seem to work reliably for me.
Do you have a simple test repeater for this?

```
$ cat <<EOF >manifest.yml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: nginx
spec:
  containers:
  - name: nginx
    image: docker.io/library/httpd:latest
    ports:
    - containerPort: 80
EOF
$ ./bin/podman play kube --detach=false ./manifest.yml
Pod:
162b6e3b85fd85ff19e9486912953089b067e1e58c11d64217139a75eb9dd2c3
Container:
10c8ae65b3b0858d9abce5365f5ed0b0dc55d72585274159431b02dbf720408a

^C$
```

For some reason SIGINT doesn't get trapped and I don't know why. The only thing I know is that I tested yesterday, and it worked. Today, a SIGINT immediately terminates without cleaning up. I am probably overlooking something huge or there's gremlins in my machine. Spent 2 hours chasing this, I'm taking a break.
Did you add a debug to see if the signal is being caught?
Shouldn't this display the output of all started container in the pod? I don't really see the purpose of this when it just wait there for the signal and does nothing.

You may be hitting the shutdown handler - https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/libpod/shutdown/handler.go

We use this to handle SIGTERM in critical sections, but it has control of what happens when Podman exits. You can register a function in it to handle actions that will happen at exit.
Thanks @1player !

> 
> 
> I expect --detach to
> 
>     * Wait until receiving SIGTERM
> 
>     * _OR_ wait until the containers and pods have exited



I would add to this that I want to get all the container output in the terminal just like `podman run`, otherwise detach would be inconsistent with other command options.

@vrothberg Just to clarify I think you mean --detach=false?  Because --detach or --detach=true should behave like it does at the moment and just exit.

> I would add to this that I want to get all the container output in the terminal just like `podman run`, otherwise detach would be inconsistent with other command options.

We could stream the logs of all containers but I fear it's too much data to be really useful for users.  I am personally OK to have "silent" --detach=false.

> @vrothberg Just to clarify I think you mean --detach=false? Because --detach or --detach=true should behave like it does at the moment and just exit.

Yes, thanks for spotting.  I updated the comment.

> > I would add to this that I want to get all the container output in the terminal just like `podman run`, otherwise detach would be inconsistent with other command options.
> 
> We could stream the logs of all containers but I fear it's too much data to be really useful for users. I am personally OK to have "silent" --detach=false.

I understand that concern but IMO it will break the user expectations if the output is missing. podman create/run/exec will all show output, same for things like `podman/docker-compose up` in all cases you have to use explicitly use --detach to not show the output.
I dislike using the same option name but with a different meaning here, that always leads to confusion.

In more general terms, as the author of the original issue that spawned this PR, I would like `--detach=false` to have similar behaviour to what `docker-compose up` does by default. That is:

1. SIGINT and SIGTERM should terminate and clean up everything.
2. Exit if pod fails to start or terminates early (though this complicates the logic somewhat: what happens when only one containers fails to start in a multi-container pod?)

Log streaming would be nice, though I suppose it can be a separate follow-up PR to this one. 

But in reality only point 1 is the main goal. I do not want to have to remember to do `podman play kube --down`, because I always forget.

Perhaps we should change from --detach to --wait, and make it positive.  So that `podman kube play --wait` Waits until all pods and containers exit. That eliminates @cdoern concern about --detach (Also I was wondering if users would ask for `attach` later.

> Perhaps we should change from --detach to --wait, 

That sounds good to me.

> [...] and make it positive. So that `podman kube play --wait` Waits until all pods and containers exit.

That would be a breaking change and would even break the new systemd template.  I think it's a sane to default to just dispatch the workload as Podman does at the moment.
Since --wait=false would be default there would be no breaking chane.

> Since --wait=false would be default there would be no breaking chane.

Yes, that would be good.  I probably misinterpreted "and make it positive".

@1player: PR needs rebase.
